title: Engineering project managers and project engineers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. (Based on 29 related US occupations.)

Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

tasks.task: Prepare schedules, reports, and estimates of the costs involved in developing and operating mines, including preparing and reviewing technical reports for engineering and management personnel.
Confer with scientific, engineering, and technical personnel to resolve design, research, and testing problems and assist engineering and other personnel to solve operating problems.
Maintain records of drilling and production operations and monitor mine production rates to assess operational effectiveness.
Develop plans for oil and gas field drilling, and for product recovery and treatment, while specifying and supervising well modification and stimulation programs to maximize oil and gas recovery.

Analyze data to recommend placement of wells and supplementary processes to enhance production, and assess costs and estimate the production capabilities and economic value of oil and gas wells to evaluate the economic viability of potential drilling sites.
Evaluate findings to develop, design, or test equipment or processes, including conducting engineering research experiments to improve or modify mining and oil machinery and operations.

Investigate or resolve operational problems, such as material use variances or bottlenecks, and conduct tests of finished products or process capabilities to establish standards or validate process requirements.
Coordinate activities of workers engaged in research, planning, and development, while coaching and supervising technicians, technologists, and engineers in their roles.

Design and implement human performance research, investigation, or analysis protocols, and develop or implement research methodologies or statistical analysis plans to test and evaluate developmental prototypes used in new products or processes.
Communicate manufacturing capabilities, production schedules, or other information to facilitate production processes, and advocate for end users in collaboration with other professionals.

Design layout of equipment or workspaces to achieve maximum efficiency while developing manufacturing methods, labor utilization standards, and cost analysis systems to promote efficient staff and facility utilization.

Evaluate current or proposed manufacturing processes or practices for environmental sustainability, while developing sustainable manufacturing technologies to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and waste.

Prepare documentation for new manufacturing processes or engineering procedures and coordinate and implement quality control objectives to resolve production problems, maximize reliability, or minimize costs.
Conduct or direct mining experiments to test or prove research findings and devise solutions to problems of land reclamation and water and air pollution.
Inspect work sites to identify physical hazards and recommend workplace changes to improve health and safety, using knowledge of potentially harmful factors.
Collect data through direct observation of work activities or witnessing the conduct of tests.

Conduct validation or qualification tests of new or existing processes, equipment, or software in accordance with internal protocols or external standards, and prepare, maintain, or review validation and compliance documentation.

Perform acceptance testing on newly installed or updated systems and conduct site analyses to determine system configurations, processes to be impacted, or on-site obstacles to technology implementation.
Develop process flows, work instructions, or standard operating procedures for radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems and determine means of integrating RFID into other applications.

Define and compare possible radio frequency identification device (RFID) solutions to inform selection for specific projects, including creating simulations or models of RFID systems to provide information for selection and configuration.

Confer with engineers, customers, vendors, or others to discuss existing or potential electronics engineering projects or products; also provide technical support or instruction to staff or customers regarding electronics equipment standards.
Install, test, or maintain radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems and select appropriate RFID tags and determine placement locations.

Evaluate project work to ensure effectiveness, technical adequacy, or compatibility in the resolution of complex electronics engineering problems while analyzing electronics system requirements, capacity, cost, or customer needs to determine project feasibility.
Conduct training or in-services to educate clinicians and other personnel on proper use of equipment and train users in details of system operation.

Develop or perform operational, maintenance, or testing procedures for electronic products, components, equipment, or systems and prepare, review, or maintain maintenance schedules, design documentation, or operational reports or charts.
Inspect electronic equipment, instruments, products, or systems to ensure conformance to specifications, safety standards, or applicable codes or regulations.
Prepare budget or cost estimates for equipment, construction, or installation projects or control expenditures, while documenting equipment or process details of RFID technology.

Design electronic components, software, products, or systems for commercial, industrial, medical, military, or scientific applications and prepare project plans for equipment or facility improvements, including time lines, budgetary estimates, or capital spending requests.
Perform systems analysis or programming of radio frequency identification device (RFID) technology.
Monitor and analyze energy consumption and conduct energy modeling, measurement, verification, commissioning, or retro-commissioning.

Conduct research or develop new green electronics technologies, such as lighting, optical data storage devices, or energy efficient televisions and investigate green consumer electronics applications.

Prepare technical reports, data summary documents, or research articles for scientific publication, regulatory submissions, or patent applications and conduct or coordinate worker training in areas such as safety laws and regulations, hazardous condition monitoring, and use of safety equipment.
Provide technical direction or support to installation teams during installation, start-up, testing, system commissioning, or performance monitoring.
Design and build safety equipment, and design medical diagnostic or clinical instrumentation, equipment, or procedures using the principles of engineering and biobehavioral sciences.

Integrate tags, readers, or software in radio frequency identification device (RFID) designs and collect data about existing client hardware, software, networking, or key business processes to inform implementation of RFID technology.
Analyze radio frequency identification device (RFID)-related supply chain data and test tags or labels to ensure readability.

Design and develop systems or technologies related to nanotechnology, photonics, energy efficiency, and robotics for applications including advanced manufacturing, environmental clean-up, renewable energy, and safety systems, while ensuring compliance with regulatory standards and optimizing performance.

Conduct harsh environmental testing, accelerated aging, device characterization, or field trials to validate devices using inspection tools, testing protocols, peripheral instrumentation, or modeling and simulation software.
Investigate mechanical failures or unexpected maintenance problems.

Conduct analyses addressing issues such as failure, reliability, or yield improvement, and analyze product failure data and laboratory test results to determine causes of problems and develop solutions.

Conduct or oversee the conduct of prototype development or microfabrication activities to ensure compliance with specifications and develop or validate product-specific test protocols, acceptance thresholds, or inspection tools for quality control testing or performance measurement.

Develop formal documentation for microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) devices, including quality assurance guidance, quality control protocols, process control checklists, data collection, or reporting, and write technical reports or other documentation such as handbooks or bulletins for use by engineering staff, management, or customers.

Create schematics and physical layouts of integrated microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) components or packaged assemblies consistent with process, functional, or package constraints; develop, coordinate, or monitor all aspects of production, including selection of manufacturing methods, fabrication, or operation of product designs.
Validate fabrication processes for microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) using statistical process control implementation, virtual process simulations, data mining, or life testing.
Modify properties of metal alloys through thermal and mechanical treatments, and alter or modify designs to obtain specified functional or operational performance.

Replicate the characteristics of materials and their components using computers, and conduct research that tests or analyzes the feasibility, design, operation, or performance of equipment, components, or systems.

Supervise production and testing processes in industrial settings, such as metal refining facilities, smelting operations, or nonmetallic materials production operations, and supervise the work of technologists, technicians, and other engineers and scientists.

Conduct training sessions on new material products, applications, or manufacturing methods for customers and their employees, and discuss plans with clients, contractors, consultants, and other engineers so that they can be evaluated and necessary changes made.
Plan and implement laboratory operations to develop material and fabrication procedures that meet cost, product specification, and performance standards.

Guide technical staff in developing materials for specific uses in projected products or devices, and consult with other engineers and corporate executives as necessary while planning and evaluating new projects.
Determine appropriate methods for fabricating and joining materials, and prepare reports, sketches, working drawings, specifications, proposals, and budgets for proposed sites or systems.
Design processing plants and equipment, and conduct or direct the testing or control of processing procedures.
Present technical information at conferences and write for technical magazines, journals, and trade association publications.
Monitor material performance and evaluate its deterioration, and evaluate technical specifications and economic factors relating to process or product design objectives.

Conduct or supervise tests on raw materials or finished products to ensure their quality, and investigate equipment failures or difficulties to diagnose faulty operation and recommend remedial actions.

Perform managerial functions such as preparing proposals and budgets, and analyze project requests, proposals, or engineering data to determine feasibility, producibility, cost, or production time of aerospace or aeronautical products.
Solving problems in a number of engineering fields, such as mechanical, chemical, electrical, civil, nuclear, or aerospace.

Meet with clients such as district or regional councils, farmers, and developers to discuss their needs, and provide advice on water quality and issues related to pollution management, river control, and ground and surface water resources.

Prepare, or direct the preparation of product or system layouts and detailed drawings and schematics, and evaluate new product plans and make recommendations for material selection based on design objectives.

Conduct environmental, operational, or performance tests on marine machinery and equipment, and evaluate performance of craft during dock and sea trials to determine design changes and conformance with standards.
Inspect marine equipment and machinery to draw up work requests and job specifications, and evaluate and approve selection of vendors by studying past performance or new advertisements.
Design structures for crop storage, animal shelter and loading, and animal and crop processing, and supervise their construction.

Design agricultural machinery components and equipment using computer-aided design (CAD) technology and conduct educational programs that provide farmers or farm cooperative members with information that can help them improve agricultural productivity.
Discuss plans with clients, contractors, consultants, and other engineers, and maintain contact with, and formulate reports for, contractors and clients to ensure completion of work at minimum cost.

Study design proposals and specifications to establish basic characteristics of craft such as size, weight, speed, propulsion, displacement, and draft, and analyze data to determine feasibility of product proposals.

Design complete hull and superstructure according to specifications and test data in conformity with standards of safety, efficiency, and economy, and evaluate product data or design from inspections or reports for conformance to engineering principles or quality standards.
Conduct research studies to develop new concepts in the field of automotive engineering.
Develop specifications for vehicles powered by alternative fuels or alternative power methods, and create design alternatives for vehicle components to increase fuel efficiency.
Design or analyze automobile systems in areas such as aerodynamics, alternate fuels, ergonomics, hybrid power, brakes, transmissions, steering, calibration, safety, or diagnostics.
Research computerized automotive applications, such as telemetrics, intelligent transportation systems, artificial intelligence, or automatic control.
Design vehicles that use lighter materials to improve fuel efficiency and conduct automotive design reviews.
Design fuel cell systems, subsystems, stacks, assemblies, or components, and analyze fuel cell or related test data, using statistical software.
Develop or implement operating methods or procedures and provide technical direction to other engineers or engineering support personnel.

Coordinate production activities with other functional units such as procurement, maintenance, or quality control, and oversee installation, operation, maintenance, or repair to ensure that machines or equipment are installed and functioning according to specifications.
Design integrated mechanical or alternative systems to improve energy efficiency, and evaluate mechanical designs or prototypes for energy performance or environmental impact.
Validate design of fuel cells, fuel cell components, or fuel cell systems.

Conduct educational programs that provide farmers or farm cooperative members with information that can help them improve agricultural productivity, and write performance requirements for product development or engineering projects.

Recommend or implement changes to fuel cell system designs and conduct evaluations of new hydrogen or non-hydrogen fuel cell system designs to assess power output, system cost, or environmental impact.

Characterize component or fuel cell performances by generating operating maps, defining operating conditions, identifying design refinements, and executing durability assessments; design or implement fuel cell testing or development programs; conduct fuel cell testing projects using fuel cell test stations and analytical instruments.

Provide technical consultation or direction related to the development or production of fuel cell systems and coordinate fuel cell engineering or test schedules with departments outside engineering, such as manufacturing.

Conduct post-service or failure analyses using electromechanical diagnostic principles or procedures; plan or conduct experiments to validate new materials and optimize startup protocols; and define specifications for fuel cell materials.
Prepare test stations, instrumentation, or data acquisition systems for use in specific tests of fuel cell components or systems, and develop fuel cell materials or fuel cell test equipment.

Plan or implement fuel cell cost reduction or product improvement projects in collaboration with other engineers, suppliers, or customers, and manage fuel cell battery hybrid system architecture, including sizing of components such as fuel cells and energy storage units.
Authorize release of fuel cell parts, components, or subsystems for production; and identify or define vehicle and system integration challenges for fuel cell vehicles.

Integrate electric drive subsystems with other vehicle systems to optimize performance or mitigate faults, and direct or coordinate activities of engineering or technical personnel involved in designing, fabricating, modifying, or testing of aircraft or aerospace products.

emerging_tasks.task: Use drone technology for aerial surveying and monitoring of drilling sites.
Use drone technology for aerial surveys and inspections of mining sites to enhance safety and efficiency.
Assess systems to identify and quantify risk factors.
Research or design methods of processing, forming, and firing materials to develop products, such as ceramic dental fillings, unbreakable dinner plates, and telescope lenses.
Review and select materials for products to meet product design and cost requirements.
Develop or modify building codes and fire codes.
Estimate energy production by analyzing wind data.
Perform testing on fire protection equipment or building features, such as fire doors or smoke control systems.
Recommend preventive measures to be taken in handling nuclear technology based on data obtained from operations monitoring, systematic analysis, or evaluation of test results.
Review building plans to verify compliance with fire and building codes.
Communicate results in peer-reviewed research articles or at workshops or conferences.
Develop and test autonomous systems for uncrewed aerospace vehicles.
Develop plans for safely drydocking vessels, including structural integrity, block loading, and stability.
Investigate equipment failures or diagnose faulty operations and recommend or perform remedial actions.
Use agricultural drones for crop monitoring, irrigation management, and pest control.
